name: python3-samba
title: python3-samba
description:
Python 3 bindings for Samba
Samba is an implementation of the SMB/CIFS protocol for Unix systems,
providing support for cross-platform file sharing with Microsoft Windows, OS X,
and other Unix systems.  Samba can also function as a domain controller
or member server in Active Directory or NT4-style domains.
.
This package contains Python 3 bindings for most Samba libraries.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use samba-tool because Python 3 bindings for Samba.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use samba-tool as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `samba-tool [-h] [-W myworkgroup] [-U user] [-d debuglevel] [-V]` as documented upstream for samba-tool.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair samba-tool with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `samba-tool --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Python 3 bindings for Samba.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: samba-tool - Main Samba administration tool.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install samba
Install with: sudo apt install samba
